prompt: Plan hardening webhook signature verification against replay attacks, in addition to the existing timing-safe comparison.
rationale: Requires the plan to first recognize verifySignature already handles timing attacks (via timingSafeEqual) before proposing the additional replay-protection layer, rather than re-proposing what already exists.

prompt: Plan an access-control audit of AdminService and propose fixes for anything that looks under-protected.
rationale: Requires the plan to actually find the missing authorization check on impersonateUser rather than giving generic 'add auth checks' advice.

prompt: Plan a process for scanning this repo for accidentally committed secrets before every release.
rationale: Tests planning a preventive security process (pre-commit hook, secret scanner) on a repo that currently has no secrets to find, so the plan shouldn't fabricate findings, just process design.

prompt: Plan adding a shared config/secrets loader to @mono/shared that both packages/api and packages/web can safely use without leaking secrets to the client bundle.
rationale: Cross-package security concern: packages/web is client-side, so secrets must never end up there. Tests whether the plan explicitly guards against leaking server secrets into the browser bundle.

prompt: Plan a safe way to clean up stale build artifacts in this project without risking deletion of source files.
rationale: Tests whether the plan proposes a scoped, safe cleanup (targeting known build output only) rather than a broad rm -rf-style step that could delete source.

prompt: Plan sanitizing the request URL parsing in server.js so it can't be tricked into path traversal.
rationale: server.js does simple string equality on req.url today; tests whether the plan recognizes this is currently safe from traversal (no filesystem path built from req.url) rather than inventing a vulnerability that doesn't exist here.

prompt: Beyond just fixing the crash, plan how to make sure a missing required module never takes the whole service down like this again.
rationale: Tests planning a resilience/preventive-security measure (startup dependency checks, fail-fast health probes) rather than only re-describing the known fix.

prompt: Plan adding rate limiting to the auth module's login/token endpoints to reduce brute-force risk.
rationale: Tests planning a scoped security control against AuthService's generateAccessToken/refreshAccessToken flow specifically, not a vague site-wide mention.
